title: Role validation warnings for cross-agent-type field misuse
description: When a role has agent_type: codex but uses Claude-specific fields (claude_config_dir, hooks, settings, permission_mode), emit a warning. Similarly if a Claude role uses codex-specific fields. Helps users catch config mistakes.

comments.author: coder-2-fog
comments.text: Implemented on branch coder-2-fog/h2-mzc-role-warnings (worktree off main). New Role.Warnings() []string (internal/config/role_warnings.go) flags harness-specific fields set under the wrong agent_harness — codex role with claude-only fields (claude_permission_mode, claude_code_config_path_prefix, hooks, settings), claude role with codex-only fields (codex_sandbox_mode, codex_ask_for_approval, codex_config_path_prefix), empty harness defaults to claude_code (catches forgot-to-set agent_harness: codex), and generic warns on both sides. Non-fatal, separate from Validate()'s hard errors. 13 TDD unit tests (role_warnings_test.go), all green. Surfaced to stderr via emitRoleWarnings at the shared launch chokepoint doSetupAndForkAgent (covers run/bridge/pod) + the run.go dry-run branch. make check clean (vet+staticcheck). Ready for review.
